MELItem.Title: Lower Cargo Compartment Fire Extinguishing Bottles

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided procedures are established and used to verify cargo compartments remain empty or contain only ballast, empty cargo handling equipment (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), or fly away kits.

OperationProcedure.Para: The lower cargo compartments must remain empty, except for ballast, empty cargo containers (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), fly away kits, pallets, and cargo restraint components.

ListItem.Para: Bottle is deactivated.
Airplane is operated pressurized.
Total fire extinguisher bottle capability is greater than or equal to maximum time to land at a suitable airport.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative fire extinguishing bottle (AMM DDG 26-23-01).

ListItem.Para: Open following circuit breakers and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag:
SMCU-CHAN 1A
SMCU-CHAN 2A
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 2B
Gain access to the inoperative bottle.
Disconnect, cap and stow the electrical connector from the squib for the associated bottle.
Put the protective cap, attached to the bottle, on the squib.
Remove the discharge line from the fire bottle discharge outlet.
Cap the discharge head of the bottle with a non-recoil cap (Kidde part number 844497).
Install a pressure holding plug (BACP20AU10D) in the discharge tube.
Close the following circuit breakers:
SMCU-CHAN 1A
SMCU-CHAN 2A
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 2B
Confirm fire bottle status message is displayed only for the deactivated bottle.

OperationProcedure.Para: Maximum time to land at suitable airport must be less than or equal to the sum of the suppression time capability for all operating low rate discharge fire extinguisher bottles. Add the suppression times for all operative bottles to obtain the maximum suppression time. The required additional 15 minutes to account for holding at the landing airport is not included in the maximum time to land at a suitable airport since it is provided by the high rate discharge bottles. The low rate discharge bottles do not begin to discharge until 15 minutes after the cargo fire suppression system is activated and the high rate bottles discharge.

Note.Para: EDTO operations may not be possible, depending on the suppression time available.

table.Title: Suppression Time per Fire Extinguisher Bottle

ListItem.Para: Bottle is deactivated.
Airplane is operated pressurized.
Total fire extinguisher bottle capability is greater than or equal to maximum time to land at a suitable airport.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative fire extinguishing bottle (AMM DDG 26-23-01).

ListItem.Para: Open following circuit breakers and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag:
SMCU-CHAN 1A
SMCU-CHAN 2A
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 2B
Gain access to the inoperative bottle.
Disconnect, cap and stow the electrical connector from the squib for the associated bottle.
Put the protective cap, attached to the bottle, on the squib.
Remove the discharge line from the fire bottle discharge outlet.
Cap the discharge head of the bottle with an non-recoil cap (Kidde part number 844497).
Install a pressure holding plug (BACP20AU10D) in the discharge tube.
Close the following circuit breakers:
SMCU-CHAN 1A
SMCU-CHAN 2A
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 2B
Confirm fire bottle status message is displayed only for the deactivated bottle.

OperationProcedure.Para: Maximum time to land at suitable airport must be less than or equal to the sum of the suppression time capability for all operating low rate discharge fire extinguisher bottles. Add the suppression times for all operative bottles to obtain the maximum suppression time. The required additional 15 minutes to account for holding at the landing airport is not included in the maximum time to land at a suitable airport since it is provided by the high rate discharge bottles. The low rate discharge bottles do not begin to discharge until 15 minutes after the cargo fire suppression system is activated and the high rate bottles discharge.

Note.Para: EDTO operations may not be possible, depending on the suppression time available.

table.Title: Suppression Time per Fire Extinguisher Bottle

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided associated bottle is verified to be fully charged.

Note.Para: The associated bottle status message will be displayed for a bottle when the bottle pressure is low or for an internal failure of the pressure monitor device when the bottle pressure is normal.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify the associated bottle is full (AMM DDG 26-23-01).

ListItem.Para: Remove, weigh and reinstall the associated bottle.
Remove the associated bottle from the airplane.
Weigh the bottle. The bottle must weigh the same as the weight on the data plate +/- 0.3 lb.

Note.Para: The measured weight of the bottle includes the charged bottle, the inspection tag on the bottle, the swivel assemblies and the two faraday caps that are connected to the pressure switch and the squib with a lanyard. If the squib cartridges are not installed on the fire extinguisher bottle, do not install them. Weigh the squib cartridges as loose parts and include the weight of the squib cartridges in the measured weight. If the squib cartridges are installed, weigh the bottle with the cartridges installed.

ListItem.Para: Reinstall the bottle in the airplane. It is not necessary to perform the bottle pressure monitor operational test associated with this procedure.
Confirm bottle pressure status message is displayed only for the bottle with the inoperative pressure transducer.
